title: Quantifying the self-discharge rate of solid-state batteries
authors: Christoph D. Alt
Jürgen Janek
abstract: Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41560-026-02038-1 Internal self-discharge limits solid-state batteries because chemical potential differences drive ions and electrons to move across the separator, causing charge loss during storage. Here Janek and Alt present an analytical model that shows how tiny electronic leaks in the solid separator can cause reversible self-discharge, providing guidance for separator and cell design.

title: A Salt-Templated Ultrathin Porous Quasi-Solid-State Electrolyte Enabling High-Voltage Lithium Metal Batteries
authors: Yvhan Shi, Jingjing Ouyang, Yan Su and Guiling Wang
abstract: Quasi-solid-state electrolytes (QSSEs) are promising for safe lithium metal batteries but limited by low conductivity and high interfacial resistance. This work proposes a lithium salt pore-forming strategy to engineer efficient ion transport channels. A porous composite electrolyte (PCE) was fabricated via solution casting and selective salt removal, using lithium bis(trifluoromethanesulfonyl)imide (LiTFSI) as a dual-functional lithium source and pore-former, poly(vinylidene fluoride-hexafluoropropylene) (PVDF-HFP) as the matrix, and garnet-type Li6.4La3Zr1.4Ta0.6O12 (LLZTO) as the inorganic …
